utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and good morning hi beverly

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good morning

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how do you see your last name

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: howard

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: howard nice to meet you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you too

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what can i do for you today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i think i like fell off of my tandem bike with my partner and my knee has been hurting

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: ever since and when did that happen

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: maybe like two weeks ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and which knee are we talking about

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my right knee

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and tell me exactly what happened

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so we were like riding together and i think he said he saw a squirrel or something like that he's a little distractible and you know like he went down so i went down i hit it on the curb like it's really kinda it was sore i gave i i took some ibuprofen and i i like washed it out and put a band-aid on it but it's it's just been like kind of hurting everyday since then so i scheduled an appointment

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay is it getting better getting worse or just kinda lingering on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's it's just holding on and i tried taking like medication right i took some ibuprofen and it was fine it just like it keeps coming back and i i read on the label i'm not supposed to take that stuff for more than two weeks right

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well it all it all depends everyone's different we'll get to that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and your knee was not bothering you at all before this normal knee

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: never okay alright what do you do for living

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well i work in a children's hospital so i like play around i i'm a i'm a a baby rocker professionally i go into like the nicu's and i i rock some of the babies

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: gotcha what do you do outside of outside of work is biking kinda your thing

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no that was his thing and i think that's why we fell i i should n't trust him but most of the time i go on walks with my dog

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay have you broke up with him already or

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i did n't know that knee injury was a reason to but now that you've told me maybe we should talk about that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we'll talk about that later too

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright so let's take a let's take a peek at your knee here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you gon na stick it through the screen or how you're gon na do this alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright then

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm just i'm gon na i'm gon na grab your leg we're just gon na take it through just let it relax i'm just gon na take it through a little range of motion i'm gon na kinda poke around all different places you tell me where it hurts okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright now i'm poking i'm poking i'm poking so on the inside part here

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is this where it hurts

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah like when you touch it it really hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: right there okay tell me what's worse here or here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: on the inside

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so medial joint line tenderness

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: pes bursa nontender epicondyle nontender back of her knee nice and soft any pain back here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i do n't think so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay popliteal space is nontender how about over on this side

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay lateral side nontender skin she has got about a dime size little abrasions dry looks about a week week old right over the medial patella everything else looks good no scars okay how about can you straighten it out all the way

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i can but sometimes it like hurts a little bit like it almost like it clicks

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yep that's good though full full extension how far can you bend it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all the way back

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay let's see

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good good and how about the other side let me see the left knee can you bend that one all the way back too

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah that's fine

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay full full symmetric flexion no no deformities go ahead and just let it relax

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: valgus stress medial collateral ligament intact lateral collateral ligament intact okay so little kind of little laxity posterior drawer let me see the other let me see the other knee

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah so that that feels pretty much normal so she's got a about a one plus posterior drawer anterior drawer negative lachman negative good let's go ahead and just kinda bend and straighten for me

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good patellar patella tracks fine no crepitus alright let's go back to this medial side you're tender right there is that right

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's tender on the medial joint line but actually negative mcmurray negative lateral side negative mcmurray good so pretty stable you you feel like you you're kinda shifting back a little bit on this side compared to the other side does it feel unstable when you walk does it feel like it shifts

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah sometimes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what's what's the worst thing that that you can do that you do n't like about that knee is it walking standing sitting still getting up from a chair in and out of the car what what do n't you like

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: walking up a hill like any kind of incline really hurts

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah and you're it's just you're weak are is it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you actually no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it hurts no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what about you i mean are you real weak

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no i would say i'm pretty strong

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: did you go to the gym

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i go to the gym but i swim so the

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay i ca n't treat people like you you you you need to work harder i quit complaining okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes sir work harder okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you do you play sports in in college or high school

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did in high school

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah so and you and you still doing anything athletic now other than just rocking kids

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just kinda you're thinking walking the dog

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah rocking kids and walking dogs and going on the occasional swim

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: on the occasional swim alright how have you been swimming since you hurt your knee

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no i have n't would you recommend that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well i'm not sure yet it's because we might have to do surgery on your knee here and take that leg off

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's pretty bad

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we'll get we'll get to that too

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so did you already have x-rays of this knee today

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay do i go into that to

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: look at the x-rays do you or just stop here you can okay okay and so what would what would happen now you would you could pause it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're only cup of this

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's take a look at your pictures here and alright so this looks pretty just looking at it looks pretty normal looks pretty healthy you can see how i all the edges of the bones here look pretty good i do n't see any bone spurs of that i do n't see anything broken everything looked looks pretty good kneecap's tracking good you can see the space here there's no real space there that's actually cartilage so that looks pretty healthy and you can see the other side looks both both look the same so x-rays look i do n't see anything obvious on that but looking at your knee here you may have hit that tibia bone and kinda pushed that bone back it feels to me like you may very well have sprained your or injured your posterior cruciate ligament that's what it's feeling like

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm not gon na know that for sure or if there is other things going on in there but i think getting an mri scan to look at what you may have done inside there i'm pretty suspicious though that you may have injured that ligament if that's the only thing you did very rarely is this gon na need surgery okay so this may be something we wan na get you into a brace here get you into some therapy and get all your motion back and get your strength back and then maybe check it back in a in a few weeks and see because a lot of times those ligaments can tighten back up

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's it's odd for just an isolated posterior ligament to need surgery

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but if there's other things going on i ca n't tell you for sure that you did n't injure a meniscus too or maybe chipped some cartilage when that bone shifted so let's let's get an mri scan to look at that if that comes back with just a ligament like i'm suspecting hopefully this is something we can treat with some therapy and some bracing for a while and get this thing back to normal if after that couple months later or so you still have instability and still bothering you then it's possible we may have to do some surgery to fix that ligament

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: does that make sense

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah so in the meantime though do you want me to still keep taking the ibuprofen

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i think that's fine for a short if it's helping the big thing right now is we want to get your strength back and your motion back get rid of the swelling that we still see here

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so that will help with that ice ice packs a few times a day you can take the ibuprofen up to three times a day and i'll look i'll we'll look at the dose here there are some other medicines we can try that maybe do n't have to take so often that may be easier

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i can even send in a prescription for you for something that maybe just twice a day a little easier for you to take without skipping doses

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay you can just take it with breakfast and dinner

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we'll try and see if it works better if your ibuprofen works better though go ahead and just go back to that but we can try this other prescription first

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's not very expensive and i can just call it into your pharmacy

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then we can we can get the brace fitted to you today we are not gon na look at a big big custom kind of expensive brace we will just put because it may just be temporary

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm mm-hmm

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then i will get you a referral to physical therapy something that you can do close to home or close to work

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then maybe i could see you back here in maybe four to six weeks and recheck this

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay how often am i gon na have to go to physical therapy

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na kinda leave that up to you and the and the therapist i would start with a couple visits and then we'll just really see how you progress and what you can do at home if what if you have access to some equipment or things that we need to do but i want you to go you know at least a few times so somebody can kinda keep an eye on you make sure you're doing the exercises right

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay alright i think that sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: did you get did you select you last night

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you yeah what do you think what do you roof for

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i so i i'm a little heart broken right now i was for the rockets and the way that these nets are crushing them it's just it's just painful

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: are you serious

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: rockets

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i live in dallas yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah yeah that's not that's not my problem though you have not do you not watch the bulls

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: my gosh alright we're gon na have to work on you

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay thanks

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: bye

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you got four to six weeks to get your attitude straight we'll talk about this later okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes sir

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright very good any questions at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so

detail_list.summary: The patient is Beverly Howard, a 33-year-old female.
The doctor asks for the patient's primary concern.
Right knee pain
The patient reports right knee pain after falling off a tandem bicycle.
The injury occurred approximately two weeks ago.
The patient hit her knee on a curb, treated it with ibuprofen and a bandage, but pain persists.
The doctor asks if the patient had any prior right knee issues.
The patient denies any previous right knee issues.
The doctor asks about the patient's occupation.
The patient works in a children's hospital as a baby rocker in the NICU.
The doctor performs a physical examination of the right knee.
The doctor identifies medial joint line tenderness.
The doctor notes the pes bursa, epicondyle, and popliteal space are nontender.
The doctor notes a dime-sized dry abrasion on the medial patella and that the lateral side is nontender.
The patient reports clicking with full extension.
The doctor notes intact collateral ligaments and 1+ posterior drawer laxity.
The doctor notes a negative Lachman test and normal patellar tracking.
The doctor confirms negative McMurray test.
The patient reports pain is worst when walking up an incline.
X-rays show no fractures or dislocations and well-preserved joint space.
The doctor suspects a posterior cruciate ligament injury.
The doctor orders an MRI to evaluate the injury.
The doctor recommends a brace and physical therapy.
The patient asks if she should continue taking ibuprofen.
The doctor advises icing the knee and continuing ibuprofen if helpful.
The doctor will prescribe a medication to be taken twice daily.
The patient will follow up in 4 to 6 weeks.
The patient asks about the frequency of physical therapy.
The doctor recommends starting with a few physical therapy visits to ensure proper exercise technique.
